title: Share who are worried about vs. share of deaths from terrorism
subtitle: Respondents who said they worried "very much" or "a great deal". Confirmed deaths, including all victims and attackers who died because of a [terrorist attack](#dod:terrorism).
caption:
Share who are worried about vs. share of deaths from terrorism
Respondents who said they worried "very much" or "a great deal". Confirmed deaths, including all victims and attackers who died because of a terrorist attack.

This scatter plot compares the share of people worried about terrorism against the share of deaths from terrorism for various countries. The vertical axis measures the share of people worried about terrorism, ranging from 30% to 90%, while the horizontal axis measures the share of deaths from terrorism on a logarithmic scale from 0.0001% to 1%. Circles are sized by the number of attacks, with a legend showing reference sizes for 200 and 500 attacks. Countries are color-coded by region: North America, South America, Africa, Europe, Asia, and Oceania. Iraq shows the highest share of deaths from terrorism at around 1%, with approximately 85% of people worried. Nigeria has around 90% of people worried with deaths from terrorism at about 0.1%. Myanmar shows the highest worry level at over 90% with deaths from terrorism around 0.01%. China has about 63% worried with deaths from terrorism at 0.0001%. The United Kingdom shows 48% worried with deaths from terrorism at 0.0001%. New Zealand has the lowest worry level at around 33% with deaths from terrorism at 0.1%. Canada shows 28% worried with deaths from terrorism at 0.001%. The Netherlands has 25% worried with deaths from terrorism at 0.001%. Countries across all regions appear throughout the chart, with no clear regional clustering visible. The data shows that higher shares of deaths from terrorism do not consistently correspond to higher shares of people worried about terrorism across the countries shown.
